shows.description: Anthology unpacking elaborate real-life scams and the con artists behind them, including a workplace fake-pregnancy scheme that spiraled into a years-long deception.
shows.notableSeasons: Kaitlyn's Baby
shows.whyNotable: Praised for tightly-plotted true-crime storytelling; part of CBC's growing scam-and-fraud narrative slate.

shows.title: Personally
shows.description: Anthology of deeply personal, single-narrator memoir documentaries; the season Creation Myth follows a woman's reckoning with her husband's certainty she'll change her mind about wanting children.
shows.notableSeasons: Creation Myth
shows.whyNotable: Part of CBC's prestige narrative-memoir slate alongside Alone: A Love Story.

shows.title: Recall: How to Start a Revolution
shows.description: Scripted history documentary revisiting Quebec's 1970 October Crisis — FLQ kidnappings, the War Measures Act, soldiers in the streets — told through bombers, survivors and officials who lived it.
shows.whyNotable: Released for the 50th anniversary of the October Crisis; praised for immersive sound design and rare archival access.

shows.title: Crime Story
shows.description: A curated true-crime documentary feed presenting longform investigative and narrative crime stories, blending CBC originals with select international true-crime documentaries.
shows.whyNotable: One of CBC's flagship true-crime aggregation feeds, regularly charting on Canadian podcast charts.

shows.title: The Detectives
shows.description: Real Canadian criminal investigations reconstructed almost entirely from actual police interrogation tapes, wiretaps and courtroom evidence, letting detectives and suspects tell the story in their own words.
shows.whyNotable: Long-running CBC Radio staple noted for its unusually raw use of primary-source audio rather than reenactment.

shows.title: On Drugs
shows.description: Scripted documentary anthology tracing the surprising, often bizarre histories of individual drugs — from caffeine to cocaine to opioids — and how they reshaped society.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for blending narrative history with science journalism; nominated for Canadian podcast awards.

shows.title: The Fifth Estate
shows.description: Canada's longest-running investigative documentary program, adapted into podcast form; each episode is a deeply reported investigation into corporate wrongdoing, institutional failure or crime.
shows.whyNotable: One of the most decorated investigative journalism programs in Canadian broadcast history.

shows.title: Ideas
shows.description: Long-running documentary program producing scripted, often single-narrator deep dives into history, science, philosophy and culture, many structured as narrative history documentaries.
shows.whyNotable: One of the longest-running and most influential documentary radio programs in the world; international radio award recognition.

shows.title: The Teacher's Pet
shows.description: Journalist Hedley Thomas re-investigates the 1982 disappearance of Sydney mother Lynette Dawson, building a case that her husband, PE teacher Chris Dawson, murdered her to be with a teenage student.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season; widely credited with reopening the police case
shows.whyNotable: One of the most downloaded true-crime podcasts globally; directly credited with prompting the reinvestigation and eventual 2022 murder conviction of Chris Dawson; Walkley and Gold Quill Award winner.

shows.title: Bronwyn
shows.description: Hedley Thomas and Claire Harvey investigate the unsolved 1993 disappearance of Bronwyn Winfield, whose husband was a member of the doomsday sect The Family led by Anne Hamilton-Byrne.
shows.whyNotable: From the team behind The Teacher's Pet; renewed police interest in the case.

shows.title: Shandee's Story
shows.description: Hedley Thomas investigates the unsolved 2013 stabbing murder of Shandee Blackburn in Mackay, Queensland, and the trial that ended in acquittal.
shows.whyNotable: Prompted a coronial inquest into the original police investigation.

shows.title: The Teacher's Trial
shows.description: Follow-up series to The Teacher's Pet, chronicling the murder trial and conviction of Chris Dawson for the killing of his wife Lynette.

shows.title: Who The Hell Is Hamish?
shows.description: Reporter David Murray unravels the double life of Hamish McLaren, a charismatic con man who posed as a globe-trotting hedge-fund trader to swindle women he met on dating apps out of millions.
shows.whyNotable: Broke a major fraud story that led to McLaren's arrest and prosecution.

shows.title: Trace
shows.description: Journalist Rachael Brown reopens the unsolved 1980 murder of Maria James, a single mother killed in her Melbourne bookshop, pursuing new forensic leads including church-linked suspects.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Won a Walkley Award; directly credited with prompting Victoria Police to reopen the case.

shows.title: Unravel True Crime
shows.description: ABC's anthology true-crime strand, with each season investigating a different unsolved Australian case in deep narrative form.
shows.notableSeasons: Blood on the Tracks (1988 death of Aboriginal teenager Mark Haines); Barrenjoey Road (1978 disappearance of Trudie Adams); Fool Me Once (an elaborate psychic con)
shows.whyNotable: Multiple Walkley Award nominations across seasons.

shows.title: Expanse
shows.description: ABC RN's narrative documentary anthology strand exploring true crimes, mysteries, and unsolved cases from regional and outback Australia.

shows.title: The Greatest Menace
shows.description: Reporter Alex Mann investigates Sydney's Chelmsford Private Hospital, where a psychiatrist's unproven 'deep sleep therapy' killed dozens of patients in the 1970s–80s despite years of warnings.
shows.whyNotable: Walkley Award-nominated; hailed as one of ABC's best-produced investigative podcasts.

shows.title: Wrong Skin
shows.description: Journalist Allan Clarke investigates the 1987 disappearance and murder of two Aboriginal teenagers, Colleen Walker and Clinton Speedy-Duroux, in Bowraville, NSW, and the decades-long fight by their families for justice.
shows.whyNotable: Credited with renewing national attention on the Bowraville murders and police failures in investigating Indigenous deaths.

shows.title: Phoebe's Fall
shows.description: Reporters Richard Baker and Michael Bachelard re-examine the 2010 death of 24-year-old Phoebe Handsjuk, who fell down a Melbourne apartment building's garbage chute, questioning the coroner's finding of misadventure.
shows.whyNotable: One of the earliest Australian narrative-investigative podcasts; prompted public scrutiny of the original inquest.

shows.title: 7am
shows.description: A daily narrative news podcast in which a single reported story — on Australian politics, power, and current affairs — is told in long-form by a journalist each morning.
shows.whyNotable: One of Australia's most popular daily news podcasts, from the publisher of The Monthly and The Saturday Paper.

shows.title: Background Briefing
shows.description: ABC RN's flagship investigative documentary program; each episode is a deeply reported feature exposing corporate wrongdoing, institutional failure, or hidden social issues.
shows.whyNotable: Decades-long institution of Australian investigative audio journalism; regular Walkley Award winner.

shows.title: Earshot
shows.description: A long-running narrative documentary strand telling immersive, character-driven true stories through rich sound design, spanning crime, social issues, and personal history.

shows.title: Days Like These
shows.description: A This American Life-style narrative nonfiction show built from true personal stories submitted by everyday Australians, produced with cinematic sound design.

shows.title: Casefile True Crime
shows.description: Anonymous-narrator series delivering meticulously researched, single-episode deep dives into true crime cases worldwide, from serial killers to bizarre unsolved mysteries.
shows.notableSeasons: The Golden State Killer episodes; the Keddie cabin murders
shows.whyNotable: One of the most popular true-crime podcasts worldwide, regularly charting in the global top 10.

shows.title: Fallout
shows.description: A narrative documentary investigating the Essendon Football Club supplements scandal, in which players were injected with an experimental, potentially banned substance under a systemic doping program.
shows.whyNotable: One of Australia's few major narrative sports-scandal podcasts.

shows.title: Bondi Badlands
shows.description: Host Patrick Abboud investigates a decades-long spate of unsolved anti-gay hate crimes and suspicious deaths at Sydney's Bondi cliffs from the 1970s to 2000s, and survivors' fight for a full police reinvestigation.
shows.whyNotable: Contributed to renewed police and public attention on the Sydney gay-hate murders inquiry.

shows.title: The Night Driver
shows.description: A narrative true-crime series reconstructing the years-long manhunt for a serial rapist terrorising Melbourne's western suburbs in the 1990s and the detectives who pursued him.
shows.whyNotable: Reclassified from unknown: LiSTNR/News Corp narrative podcasts are distributed free, ad-supported, on all major apps like other iHeart/indie shows.

shows.title: Fool Me Once
shows.description: Investigates an elaborate, long-running psychic con that manipulated and defrauded a family over years, exposing the mechanics of coercive control by a self-styled clairvoyant.
shows.whyNotable: Standalone-notable season of the Unravel anthology.

shows.title: Scam Inc
shows.description: Investigative narrative series tracing the global rise of industrial-scale "pig butchering" scam compounds, from trafficked scam-center workers to the crypto laundering networks that profit from them.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 on pig-butchering compounds in Southeast Asia
shows.whyNotable: Widely praised as one of The Economist's most successful audio ventures, spawning multiple seasons on different scam networks. Feed note: base episodes free with ads on public RSS; Economist Podcasts+ unlocks ad-free/early access.

shows.title: The Prince
shows.description: A narrative profile of Xi Jinping tracing his rise to power and the forces that shaped modern China, told through reporting and historical reconstruction.
shows.whyNotable: Banned in China; one of The Economist's flagship narrative audio projects, later followed by companion series on other world leaders (The Prince: Searching for Putin, MBS). Feed note: free with ads on public RSS; Economist Podcasts+ unlocks ad-free/early access.

shows.title: Boss Class
shows.description: Narrative-driven exploration of management and workplace power dynamics through real case studies and reporting.
shows.whyNotable: Feed note: free with ads on public RSS; Economist Podcasts+ unlocks ad-free/early access.

shows.title: Untold: The Retreat
shows.description: An FT investigative narrative series examining a wellness retreat and the fallout from allegations against its leader.
shows.whyNotable: Part of FT's push into scripted narrative investigative audio.

shows.title: Hot Money: The New Narcos
shows.description: A narrative investigation into the cocaine trade's modern financial infrastructure and the people profiting from it, from cartels to legitimate-seeming businesses.
shows.notableSeasons: Season on cocaine trafficking finance; also covered sports betting fraud
shows.whyNotable: FT's Hot Money franchise won acclaim for blending financial journalism with narrative crime storytelling.

shows.title: Hot Money: The Case Against Neymar
shows.description: Narrative deep-dive into fraud allegations and financial dealings surrounding the football superstar's transfer.

shows.title: Foundering
shows.description: Narrative documentary series from tech-business outlet The Information reconstructing the rise and dramatic collapse of a single startup per season through interviews and reporting.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 on IRL (fake user numbers scandal)
shows.whyNotable: Bloomberg's flagship narrative business-collapse podcast about the failed startup IRL.

shows.title: The Shift
shows.description: Narrative business journalism examining major shifts and upheavals inside companies and industries, reported like a serialized drama.

shows.title: Fortune Favors the Bold
shows.description: Narrative series tracing an entrepreneur or company through triumph and disaster in pursuit of ambitious business goals.

shows.title: Bloomberg Originals: Elon Inc.
shows.description: Ongoing narrative and analytical coverage of Elon Musk's business empire, tracing decisions and controversies across his companies.

shows.title: Man in the Window: The Golden State Killer
shows.description: Narrative true-crime series retracing the decades-long hunt for the Golden State Killer, built around LA Times reporter Paige St. John's original reporting and the DNA genealogy breakthrough that cracked the case.
shows.whyNotable: Credited with helping popularize genetic genealogy in cold-case journalism; widely acclaimed narrative true-crime series.

shows.title: Detective Trapp
shows.description: Narrative series following a Sacramento detective's obsessive pursuit of a serial rapist, and the flawed police investigation that let him operate for years.
shows.whyNotable: From the Dirty John team at LA Times; explores institutional failure in sexual assault investigations.

shows.title: Room 20
shows.description: Narrative investigation into an unidentified murder victim and the decades-long effort by a small-town coroner to identify her and find her killer.

shows.title: Chasing Cosby
shows.description: Narrative reported history of the decades of allegations against Bill Cosby, and how the justice system and media failed his accusers for years.
shows.whyNotable: Co-produced with Wondery; ad-free/early listening sits behind a Wondery+ subscription.

shows.title: Slow Burn
shows.description: Season-length narrative histories that revisit major American political and cultural scandals episode by episode, reconstructed largely from the participants' own words.
shows.whyNotable: One of the most acclaimed narrative political-history podcasts; Slate's flagship narrative franchise.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (Watergate), Season 2 (Clinton impeachment), Season 3 (Biggie & Tupac), Season 5 (David Duke)

shows.title: Standoff: What Happened at Ruby Ridge?
shows.network: Oregon Public Broadcasting / distributed w/ press partners
shows.description: Narrative reconstruction of the 1992 Ruby Ridge siege and its role in fueling the American militia movement.

shows.title: Black Box
shows.description: Narrative investigation into a plane crash and the hidden human and corporate failures behind it.
shows.whyNotable: Part of The Guardian's expansion into scripted investigative narrative audio.

shows.title: Can I Tell You a Secret?
shows.description: Narrative investigation into a years-long catfishing and stalking campaign and the police failures that let it continue.
shows.whyNotable: Based on Guardian/BBC reporting; also adapted into a Netflix documentary.

shows.title: Redhanded Presents / Today in Focus: The Coup
shows.description: Narrative multi-part investigation format used periodically by The Guardian's Today in Focus to tell single sustained stories, such as attempted coups and corporate scandals, across several episodes.

shows.title: The Turning: The Sisters Who Left
shows.description: Narrative investigation into a controversial dance ministry and the women who left it, and the ones who came after.
shows.whyNotable: Distributed with reporting ties to The Atlantic; explored coercive control within a religious dance group.

shows.title: Radio Atlantic: The Battle for the Mormon Church
shows.description: Narrative reported series on doctrinal upheaval and power struggles within the LDS Church.
shows.whyNotable: Companion audio to McKay Coppins' Atlantic cover investigation.

shows.title: We Live Here Now
shows.description: Narrative true-crime series about a couple whose dream home purchase unravels a web of murder and fraud connected to the property's past owner.

shows.title: Bear Brook
shows.description: Investigative series that starts with four unidentified bodies found in barrels in Bear Brook State Park and follows the DNA-genealogy breakthroughs that helped crack the case, later spinning into related cold-case seasons.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (the original Bear Brook murders); Bear Brook: Sons of Sam & David Meirhofer season
shows.whyNotable: Peabody Award winner; credited with popularizing forensic genetic genealogy in true crime journalism.

shows.title: In the Dark
shows.description: Rigorous investigative journalism series. Season one examines the Jacob Wetterling abduction and police failures; season two dismantles the case against Curtis Flowers, a Mississippi man tried six times for the same murder.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 2 (Curtis Flowers case)
shows.whyNotable: Two-time Peabody Award winner; Season 2 won the Pulitzer Prize for Audio Reporting and directly contributed to Flowers's exoneration.

shows.title: Your Own Backyard
shows.description: Independently produced investigation into the 1996 disappearance of Kristin Smart from Cal Poly, combining original reporting with community tips that helped reopen the case.
shows.whyNotable: Credited with generating public pressure and new evidence that led to the eventual arrest and conviction of Paul Flores.

shows.title: Root of Evil: The True Story of the Hodel Family and the Black Dahlia
shows.description: Two granddaughters of Dr. George Hodel investigate his alleged connection to the unsolved 1947 Black Dahlia murder and other family secrets, blending personal memoir with cold-case journalism.
shows.notableSeasons: Single-season arc
shows.whyNotable: Praised for its intimate first-person access to a decades-old, high-profile unsolved case.

shows.title: Bardstown
shows.description: Narrative investigation into two unsolved murders in the small town of Bardstown, Kentucky, examining how the killings fractured the community and whether local law enforcement mishandled the cases.
shows.whyNotable: From the Tenderfoot TV team (Up and Vanished, Atlanta Monster); noted for deep small-town access.

shows.title: To Live and Die in LA
shows.description: Investigative series following journalist Neil Strauss's real-time search for missing actress Adea Shabani in Los Angeles, capturing the reporting process as it unfolds.
shows.whyNotable: Notable for its real-time, in-progress investigative format rather than retrospective storytelling.

shows.title: Sweet Bobby
shows.description: Journalist Alexi Mostrous investigates a years-long catfishing scheme that psychologically entrapped a London woman under the guise of a boyfriend named 'Bobby'.
shows.whyNotable: British Podcast Award winner; later adapted into the Netflix documentary 'Sweet Bobby'.

shows.title: West Cork
shows.description: Investigation into the unsolved 1996 murder of French film producer Sophie Toscan du Plantier in a remote Irish village, examining the prime suspect and the case's lingering shadow over the community.
shows.whyNotable: Critically acclaimed Audible Original (Audible subscription/credit required to hear it); later the subject of a Netflix documentary series.

shows.title: Up and Vanished
shows.description: Filmmaker Payne Lindsey investigates cold missing-persons cases, beginning with the 2005 disappearance of Georgia beauty queen Tara Grinstead.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (Tara Grinstead)
shows.whyNotable: Breakout hit credited with generating tips that led to arrests in the Grinstead case; launched Tenderfoot TV as a major true-crime studio.

shows.title: Atlanta Monster
shows.description: Narrative re-investigation of the Atlanta Child Murders of 1979–1981 and the conviction of Wayne Williams, questioning whether the right man was caught.
shows.whyNotable: Highly rated Tenderfoot TV series; sparked renewed public debate over Wayne Williams's conviction.

shows.title: Slow Burn (Season on crime/scandal entries)
shows.description: Narrative history series that reconstructs major American scandals and crimes season by season with archival tape and contemporaneous interviews, several seasons centered on murder and political corruption.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 4 (David Duke); Season 8 (Kanye West era, less crime-focused)
shows.whyNotable: Critically acclaimed for immersive scene-setting; widely cited as a model for narrative history podcasting.

shows.title: Alphabet Boys
shows.description: Investigative anthology using leaked recordings and documents to examine controversial U.S. law-enforcement informant operations and sting operations gone wrong.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (Denver Ferguson protest sting)
shows.whyNotable: Noted for rare access to raw undercover recordings that other outlets couldn't obtain.

shows.title: Bellwether
shows.description: A serialized fiction thriller presented true-crime-style, following a true-crime podcaster who becomes entangled in the disappearance she's investigating in a small town.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season, self-contained arc
shows.whyNotable: From audiochuck (Crime Junkie's network), notable as a scripted fiction title from a network built on nonfiction true crime, deliberately blurring the line.

shows.title: The Black Tapes
shows.network: Pacific Northwest Stories / Minnow Beats Whale
shows.description: A mockumentary in the mold of Serial: journalist Alex Reagan profiles paranormal debunker Dr. Richard Strand and his 'black tapes' of unsolved supernatural cases, only to be drawn into the mystery herself.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's central Strand/black-tapes arc
shows.whyNotable: One of the breakout post-Serial fiction podcasts; widely credited with popularizing the investigative-journalism-as-horror-fiction format and spawned the spinoff Tanis.

shows.title: Tanis
shows.network: Pacific Northwest Stories / Minnow Beats Whale
shows.description: Host Nic Silver investigates the recurring myth of 'Tanis,' a possibly mystical place referenced across centuries of conspiracy theory, folklore, and disappearances.
shows.notableSeasons: Seasons 1–2 core mythology arc
shows.whyNotable: Companion series to The Black Tapes from creator Terry Miles; a foundational title in the fiction-podcast mockumentary wave.

shows.title: Limetown
shows.description: Public radio journalist Lia Haddock investigates the unexplained disappearance of over 300 people from a research facility town a decade earlier.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's original disappearance investigation
shows.whyNotable: iTunes/Apple Podcasts breakout hit and Editor's Pick; adapted into a scripted TV series (Facebook Watch, later Spotify) starring Jessica Biel.

shows.title: The Message
shows.description: A team of cryptologists and a tech journalist race to decode an alien radio signal, only to discover it may be a dangerous weapon disguised as a message.
shows.notableSeasons: Single 8-episode season
shows.whyNotable: GE-sponsored branded fiction that hit #1 on the iTunes charts in 2015, praised for sound design and helping prove branded audio fiction could be a hit format.

shows.title: LifeAfter
shows.description: After a young woman's death, her digital assistant continues speaking in her voice, drawing her sister and a journalist into a mystery about AI, grief, and corporate motives.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Follow-up to The Message from the same GE-sponsored fiction lab; an early, well-reviewed exploration of AI-and-grief themes in audio fiction.

shows.title: Within the Wires
shows.description: Told through found audio artifacts — relaxation tapes, museum audio guides, personal recordings — that gradually reveal a dystopian surveillance society and the fractured relationships within it.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (relaxation tapes) and Season 4 (museum guide) are standout formal experiments
shows.whyNotable: From Welcome to Night Vale creators; critically acclaimed for its unconventional second-person 'document' storytelling structure.

shows.title: Rabbits
shows.description: A woman searching for a missing friend is pulled into 'Rabbits,' a secretive, possibly centuries-old alternate reality game with real-world stakes.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 2 expands the mythology of the underground game
shows.whyNotable: From Black Tapes/Tanis creator Terry Miles; praised for intricate ARG-style puzzle plotting and worldbuilding.

shows.title: Passenger List
shows.description: A college student investigates the disappearance of a transatlantic flight her twin brother was on, uncovering a cover-up as she digs through conflicting witness accounts.
shows.notableSeasons: Single 8-episode season
shows.whyNotable: Starred Kelly Marie Tran; a Radiotopia/Panoply co-production that drew strong reviews for its slow-burn investigative structure.

shows.title: The Amelia Project
shows.description: An anthology in which a mysterious agency interviews clients before helping them fake their own deaths and disappear into new lives, each episode framed as an in-world interview or case file.
shows.notableSeasons: Ongoing anthology; no single arc, strength is episodic variety
shows.whyNotable: Multiple British Podcast Award and Audioverse Award wins; a critical darling of the independent fiction-podcast scene.

shows.title: We're Alive
shows.description: A group of survivors record their experiences banding together in a Los Angeles apartment complex during a zombie apocalypse, structured like serialized survivor testimony.
shows.notableSeasons: Seasons 1–3 core apartment-complex survival arc
shows.whyNotable: One of the earliest and longest-running independent audio drama hits, predating the Serial-era fiction-podcast boom and building a large devoted fanbase.

shows.title: Homecoming
shows.description: Told through counseling-session recordings and a Department of Defense audit years later, the series follows a caseworker at a facility helping soldiers transition home, and the sinister purpose beneath it.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's original two-timeline structure
shows.whyNotable: Starred Oscar Isaac and Catherine Keener; adapted into an Amazon Prime Video series starring Julia Roberts, one of the highest-profile podcast-to-TV adaptations.

shows.title: Alice Isn't Dead
shows.description: A long-haul trucker records audio diaries while searching cross-country for her wife, presumed dead, encountering a monstrous, conspiratorial version of America along the way.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's search/discovery arc
shows.whyNotable: From Welcome to Night Vale creator Joseph Fink; well-reviewed for its road-trip audio-diary format and later adapted into a novel.

shows.title: Archive 81
shows.description: An audio archivist hired to restore water-damaged tapes discovers they document a documentary filmmaker's investigation into a haunted apartment building, and starts experiencing the same phenomena.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's original tape-restoration mystery
shows.whyNotable: Adapted into a Netflix live-action series in 2022; a well-regarded indie entry in the found-footage/found-audio subgenre.

shows.title: Video Palace
shows.description: A film scholar investigates a cursed VHS tape sent to him anonymously, structured as a documentary podcast tracking down the tape's origins and victims.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: A found-footage-style horror fiction podcast praised for its slow-build documentary framing device.

shows.title: Steal the Stars
shows.description: Framed as internal recordings and interrogation audio, a security officer at a secret facility housing a crashed alien vessel falls in love and plots to steal the extraterrestrial body inside.
shows.notableSeasons: Single 14-episode season
shows.whyNotable: One of the first major fiction podcasts from a book publisher (Tor); later adapted into a novel by the same author.

shows.title: Ars Paradoxica
shows.description: Presented through the recovered audio logs of a secret 1940s government time-travel research program and the physicist accidentally flung through time who becomes its architect.
shows.notableSeasons: Seasons 1–3 covering the full time-travel-program history
shows.whyNotable: Audioverse Award winner, a favorite among independent fiction-podcast listeners for its structural ambition and voice acting.

shows.title: The White Vault
shows.description: Presented as recovered audio logs from an international research team's expedition to a remote Arctic research station, which uncovers something buried beneath the ice.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's original Svalbard expedition arc
shows.whyNotable: A long-running, highly rated horror-fiction podcast from the makers of The Amelia Project, known for immersive multilingual sound design.

shows.title: Case 63
shows.years: 2022 (English adaptation); originated as Chile's El Hilo Rojo
shows.description: A psychiatrist's recorded therapy sessions with a patient who claims to be a scientist sent back in time to prevent a global catastrophe, framed as clinical case-file audio.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Starred Julianne Moore and Oscar Isaac; a Spotify Original built from an international hit format. Feed note: Spotify-exclusive, no open RSS.

shows.title: Sandra
shows.description: A journalist investigates the tech company behind Sandra, a wildly popular AI voice assistant, uncovering the humans secretly powering its 'artificial' intelligence.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Starred Kristen Wiig; praised for satirizing tech-industry journalism conventions while telling a speculative story.

shows.title: Blackout
shows.description: Framed as a radio DJ's real-time broadcast during a mysterious, escalating New York City blackout that turns increasingly ominous and supernatural.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Starred Rami Malek; part of QCODE's push into star-driven scripted fiction podcasts with eventual TV/film adaptation ambitions.

shows.title: The Cipher
shows.description: A true-crime podcaster investigating a decade-old disappearance becomes an unwitting participant in the case herself, told as a podcast-within-a-podcast.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Notable for its meta structure directly mimicking real true-crime investigative podcasts.

shows.title: Old Gods of Appalachia
shows.description: A cosmic-horror alternate history of Appalachia, narrated in the register of regional folklore and oral history documentary rather than straight drama.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1's founding mythos arc
shows.whyNotable: Breakout indie horror-fiction hit known for its distinctive folklore-documentary narration style and large fan community.

shows.title: Wolverine: The Long Night
shows.description: An FBI agent investigates a string of brutal murders in a small Alaskan town, presented as a true-crime-style audio investigation that gradually reveals a Marvel superhero connection.
shows.notableSeasons: Single 10-episode season
shows.whyNotable: Starred Richard Armitage, hit #1 on Apple Podcasts charts; a high-profile Marvel/Stitcher fiction crossover using true-crime podcast conventions. Distributed free on all major podcast apps at launch, not gated behind a paywall.

shows.title: You're Wrong About
shows.description: Long-form reexaminations of misremembered news stories, moral panics, and public figures, correcting the popular record with reported detail.
shows.notableSeasons: Series on the Satanic Panic, Tonya Harding, and the O.J. Simpson trial
shows.whyNotable: Breakout indie hit; frequently named one of the most influential podcasts of the late 2010s revisionist-history wave.

shows.title: Blowback
shows.description: Deeply researched, scripted seasonal histories of U.S. foreign policy interventions, combining archival reporting with sharp narrative argument.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (Iraq War), Season 2 (Cuba/Bay of Pigs), Season 5 (Korean War)
shows.whyNotable: Critically acclaimed indie foreign-policy history series, praised for depth of archival sourcing.

shows.title: You Must Remember This
shows.description: Narrative history of 20th-century Hollywood exploring the forgotten and mythologized stories of the studio era, scripted single-narrator format.
shows.notableSeasons: "Charles Manson's Hollywood," "Six Degrees of Song of the South," "Polly Platt: The Invisible Woman"
shows.whyNotable: Widely regarded as a landmark of narrative entertainment-history podcasting; extensive critical acclaim.

shows.title: History on Fire
shows.description: Daniele Bolelli narrates sweeping, single-voice accounts of historical conflicts, revolutions, and legendary figures with novelistic pacing.
shows.notableSeasons: Multi-part series on the Haitian Revolution and Alexander the Great
shows.whyNotable: Long-running fan-favorite in the solo-narrator military/history genre.

shows.title: The Constant: A History of Getting Things Wrong
shows.description: Single-narrator deep dives into historical errors, failed ideas, and forgotten mistakes, from doomed expeditions to discredited science.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for meticulous research and dry wit; cult favorite in the history-podcast community.

shows.title: Empire
shows.description: Narrative-leaning history series (hosted format with heavy scripted narrative segments) tracing the rise and fall of historical empires across eras.
shows.notableSeasons: Series on the Mughal Empire, the British in India, and the Roman Empire
shows.whyNotable: One of the UK's top-charting history podcasts; from the makers of The Rest Is History network.

shows.title: Long Lost
shows.description: Serialized narrative journalism following the host's own investigation into her family's hidden history and a decades-old secret.
shows.notableSeasons: Single-season arc
shows.whyNotable: Critically praised for blending personal memoir with reported historical investigation.

shows.title: The Dropout
shows.description: Investigative narrative series tracing Elizabeth Holmes's founding of Theranos, the fraud that unraveled it, and the subsequent criminal trial.
shows.notableSeasons: Single-season arc that later became the basis for the Hulu series of the same name
shows.whyNotable: One of the most influential corporate-fraud narrative podcasts; spawned a hit TV adaptation.

shows.title: Scene on Radio
shows.description: Narrative documentary seasons examining the history and structures of race, gender, and democracy in America, narrated by John Biewen.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 2 ("Seeing White") and Season 4 ("The Land That Never Has Been Yet") are landmark, widely taught seasons
shows.whyNotable: Extremely influential in public discourse on race in America; widely used in classrooms.

shows.title: Ghost Church
shows.description: Investigative narrative journalism into a charismatic televangelist and the secretive, abusive dynamics of the megachurch he built.
shows.notableSeasons: Single-season arc
shows.whyNotable: Critically noted addition to the religious high-control-group narrative genre; free with ads, with ad-free/early episodes via Wondery+.

shows.title: Mystery Show
shows.description: Starlee Kine investigates small, quirky real-life mysteries brought to her by friends and listeners, told in a wandering first-person narrative style.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (the only season); the 'Belt Buckle' and Britney Spears library-card episodes
shows.whyNotable: Critically adored cult favorite; its abrupt cancellation after one season became podcast-industry lore.

shows.title: Serial (Season 1)
shows.description: Sarah Koenig re-investigates the 1999 murder of Hae Min Lee and the conviction of Adnan Syed, unspooling the case week by week in real time.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (Adnan Syed case)
shows.whyNotable: The show credited with popularizing podcasting itself; fastest podcast ever to reach 5 million downloads at the time; directly influenced the Syed case's later legal proceedings.

shows.title: Undisclosed
shows.network: Independent (Rabia Chaudry, Susan Simpson, Colin Miller)
shows.description: Legal-analysis-driven investigative podcast that picked up where Serial left off on the Adnan Syed case, then expanded to cover other contested convictions.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1: Adnan Syed; later seasons on Joey Watkins and other wrongful-conviction cases
shows.whyNotable: Run by practicing attorneys, credited with contributing evidence that influenced Adnan Syed's legal appeals; a foundational entry in the wrongful-conviction podcast subgenre.

shows.title: Accused
shows.description: Investigative journalism podcast reopening cold-case murders, starting with the decades-unsolved killing of Elizabeth Andes.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1: Elizabeth Andes case
shows.whyNotable: Local-newspaper-produced investigation that helped reopen a decades-cold case; an early example of a regional paper breaking into national narrative podcasting.

shows.title: Sword and Scale
shows.description: Raw, unflinching true-crime documentary series built from archival 911 calls, interrogation tapes, and court audio with minimal narration.
shows.notableSeasons: Early seasons (1–4) before the show's later hiatus/relaunch
shows.whyNotable: One of the first breakout independent true-crime podcasts, known for its stark, narrator-light documentary style; helped kick off the true-crime podcast boom alongside Serial.

shows.title: Lore
shows.description: Narrated deep dives into the real history behind folklore, myths, and legends, told with a documentary storytelling approach.
shows.notableSeasons: Early folklore-history episodes that led to the Amazon TV adaptation
shows.whyNotable: One of the first indie narrative history podcasts to break into the iTunes top charts; adapted into an Amazon Prime anthology series.

shows.title: Missing & Murdered: Who Killed Alberta Williams?
shows.description: Investigative journalist Connie Walker probes the unsolved 1989 murder of a young Indigenous woman along Canada's 'Highway of Tears.'
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1
shows.whyNotable: Landmark Indigenous-led investigative journalism; won a Peabody Award and helped spotlight Canada's missing and murdered Indigenous women crisis.

shows.title: Man in the Window
shows.description: Reported serialized account tracing the decades-long hunt for the Golden State Killer, told alongside the case's real-time DNA-led resolution.
shows.notableSeasons: Single season
shows.whyNotable: Produced by the LA Times team that had tracked the case for years; released as the arrest broke, drawing major national attention.

shows.title: Suave
shows.description: A decades-spanning portrait of David 'Suave' Gonzalez, sentenced to life as a teenager, and his fight for release and reentry.
shows.whyNotable: Pulitzer Prize for Audio Reporting finalist; Peabody nominee.

shows.title: Floodlines
shows.description: An eight-episode narrative history of Hurricane Katrina told through the people who lived it, examining who the government protected and who it failed.
shows.whyNotable: Peabody Award winner.

shows.title: Reveal
shows.network: The Center for Investigative Reporting & PRX
shows.description: Weekly investigative documentary journalism covering corporate malfeasance, government failure, and abuse of power.
shows.whyNotable: Multiple Peabody Awards and Third Coast Awards; reporting has driven Pulitzer-winning print investigations.

shows.title: Alaska's Missing and Murdered
shows.description: An investigation into the epidemic of missing and murdered Indigenous people in Alaska and the institutional failures behind it.
shows.whyNotable: Peabody Award winner.

shows.title: Long Shadow
shows.description: An investigative narrative series probing the causes and long tail of the September 11 attacks and the wars that followed.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (origins of 9/11)
shows.whyNotable: Pulitzer Prize for Audio Reporting finalist.

shows.title: Bone Valley
shows.description: A reinvestigation of the murder conviction of Leo Schofield in Florida, built around new evidence pointing to a different suspect.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1
shows.whyNotable: Ambies nominee; Peabody nominee; credited with contributing to renewed legal review of the case.

shows.title: Dolly Parton's America
shows.description: A reported narrative exploration of Dolly Parton's life, myth, and cross-partisan appeal in American culture, told through her music and the people around her.
shows.whyNotable: Peabody Award nominee; Ambies recognition.

shows.title: Death of an Artist
shows.description: A narrative investigation into the 1985 death of artist Ana Mendieta and the art-world silence that followed her husband Carl Andre's trial.
shows.whyNotable: Ambies nominee; widely reviewed as a standout narrative art-world investigation.

shows.title: Empire on Blood
shows.description: A reinvestigation of a wrongful murder conviction in the Bronx built on a jailhouse informant's testimony.
shows.whyNotable: Critically acclaimed narrative wrongful-conviction investigation; awards-circuit nominee.

shows.title: Missing & Murdered: Finding Cleo
shows.description: Investigates the disappearance of an Indigenous Canadian girl taken during the Sixties Scoop, and her family's decades-long search.
shows.whyNotable: Peabody Award winner.

shows.title: Heaven's Gate
shows.description: A close reading of the Heaven's Gate UFO religion's rise and mass suicide, told with unusual empathy for its members rather than mockery.
shows.whyNotable: Widely praised as a template for humane cult journalism; frequently cited on best-of lists.

shows.title: Sold a Story
shows.description: Investigative narrative series exposing how a debunked reading-instruction method persisted in American schools for decades, driven by a publishing industry that profited from it despite the science.
shows.whyNotable: Won a Peabody Award and prompted state-level legislative reform of reading instruction.

shows.title: The Competition
shows.description: Investigates a suspicious cluster of deaths and disappearances tied to a prestigious classical music competition circuit.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for atmospheric, novelistic reporting.

shows.title: Normal Gossip
shows.description: Anonymized, narratively told everyday gossip stories submitted by listeners, read and unpacked with a guest each episode.
shows.whyNotable: Breakout indie hit; topped Apple Podcasts charts as a completely independent production.

shows.title: Do Go On
shows.description: Long-running Australian indie storytelling show blending narrative history and personal essay episodes.
shows.whyNotable: One of Australia's longest-running independent narrative podcasts.

shows.title: Broken Harts
shows.description: Investigates the murder-suicide of the Hart family, two mothers who drove their six adopted children off a cliff, and the child-welfare warning signs that were missed along the way.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for its investigation into failures of the child welfare system. Feed note: free with ads on the standard RSS feed; Wondery+ required for ad-free/early episodes.

shows.title: Runaway Princesses
shows.description: Follows the stories of Dubai royal family daughters who tried to flee, and the systems that kept them trapped.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for deeply reported access to a closed royal world.

shows.title: Root of Evil
shows.description: A journalist teams up with the granddaughter of prime suspect Dr. George Hodel to re-investigate the unsolved 1947 Black Dahlia murder in Los Angeles.
shows.whyNotable: Praised for blending family memoir with cold-case investigative journalism.

shows.title: The Turnaround
shows.description: Public radio host Jesse Thorn interviews other renowned interviewers about their craft, philosophy, and most memorable moments on the mic.
shows.whyNotable: Not narrative true-crime style; included as an interview-format outlier praised for its insight into the craft of interviewing.

shows.title: Gangster Capitalism
shows.description: Anthology series on the business empires built by organized crime figures, from opioid traffickers to Escobar's cartel.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1: The Sackler family and OxyContin
shows.whyNotable: Praised for tying true crime to systemic corporate wrongdoing.

shows.title: Dying for Sex
shows.description: A terminally ill woman's audio diary of leaving her marriage to pursue sexual liberation before she dies, told through recorded conversations with her best friend.
shows.whyNotable: Adapted into a Hulu limited series starring Michelle Williams. Feed note: free with ads on the standard RSS feed; Wondery+ required for ad-free/early episodes.

shows.title: Anatomy of Doubt
shows.description: Reconstructs how police wrongly charged a rape victim with lying, and the investigative reporting that vindicated her years later.
shows.whyNotable: Basis for the Pulitzer-winning 'An Unbelievable Story of Rape' and the Netflix series Unbelievable.

shows.title: El hilo
shows.description: Weekly narrative news podcast in Spanish that unpacks one major current-events story from Latin America each week through deep reporting and storytelling.
shows.notableSeasons: Coverage of the Chilean social uprising; the Colombian peace process; the fall of Peru's presidents
shows.whyNotable: Sister show to Radio Ambulante; widely cited as essential listening for understanding Latin American current affairs in narrative form.

shows.title: Las Raras
shows.description: A limited-run narrative series in Spanish profiling unusual and lesser-told stories of women across Latin America whose lives defied convention.
shows.notableSeasons: Full single season profiling pioneering and rebellious Latin American women
shows.whyNotable: Praised as an intimate, feminist-inflected companion series to Radio Ambulante.

shows.title: Se Regalan Dudas
shows.description: Narrative and reflective storytelling in Spanish exploring mental health, trauma, and personal transformation through real listener and host stories.
shows.whyNotable: One of the most downloaded Spanish-language podcasts in Latin America.

shows.title: Ábrete Sésamo
shows.description: Narrative true-crime and mystery storytelling in Spanish focused on unsolved and strange cases from Spain and Latin America.
shows.whyNotable: A leading Spanish true-crime narrative show from Spain's Podium Podcast network.

shows.title: Alkaid
shows.description: Narrative deep-dive series in Spanish reconstructing famous and obscure true crime cases with cinematic scripted narration.
shows.whyNotable: Flagship narrative true-crime show of Podium Podcast, Spain's largest podcast network.

shows.title: Transfert
shows.description: French-language narrative documentary anthology in which each episode tells a single first-person true story — secrets, crimes, and life-altering events — in dramatized, reported form.
shows.notableSeasons: Episodes on cult survival, family secrets, and criminal confessions
shows.whyNotable: One of France's most popular independent narrative podcasts; helped establish Binge Audio as a major French podcast network.

shows.title: Injustices
shows.description: French narrative investigative series re-examining real criminal cases and possible miscarriages of justice in France.
shows.notableSeasons: Season on the Grégory Villemin murder case (l'affaire Grégory)
shows.whyNotable: A landmark French true-crime narrative series revisiting one of France's most infamous unsolved cases.

shows.title: Legacy (Affaires Sensibles spin-offs) / Ou peut-être une nuit
shows.description: ARTE Radio narrative documentary in French recounting a woman's decades-long quest to understand a violent assault in her family's past.
shows.notableSeasons: Single acclaimed season
shows.whyNotable: Winner of the Prix Europa and widely acclaimed as a landmark of French narrative audio documentary.

shows.title: Mécaniques du complotisme
shows.description: French narrative documentary series investigating the mechanics and real-world consequences of conspiracy theories through personal stories.
shows.whyNotable: Acclaimed ARTE Radio documentary series; part of ARTE Radio's award-winning narrative documentary slate.

shows.title: Espions
shows.description: French narrative series telling true stories of spies, espionage, and intelligence operations through reported storytelling.
shows.whyNotable: A leading French-language narrative espionage documentary podcast.

shows.title: Zeit Verbrechen
shows.description: German narrative true-crime journalism podcast in which Zeit journalists narrate deeply reported real criminal cases from Germany and beyond.
shows.notableSeasons: Episodes on the NSU neo-Nazi murders and major German cold cases
shows.whyNotable: One of Germany's most acclaimed narrative true-crime podcasts, produced by the respected Die Zeit newsroom.

shows.title: Zeit Verbrechen: Mordlust-style narrative spin-offs / Fall Eins
shows.description: German narrative documentary podcast reconstructing a single real criminal case per season through scripted narration and original reporting.
shows.notableSeasons: Season on a wrongful-conviction case in Germany
shows.whyNotable: Praised by German critics as a genre-defining narrative true-crime production from public broadcaster NDR.

shows.title: Deutschland3000 narrative specials / Wunder
shows.description: German-language narrative documentary series from Wondery's German arm reconstructing real crimes, scandals, and cons with dramatized narration.
shows.whyNotable: Part of Wondery's international expansion bringing its scripted narrative true-crime format to the German market. Feed note: ad-free/early episodes sit behind Wondery+/RTL+.

shows.title: Cui Bono: WirecardStory
shows.description: German narrative documentary series unraveling the collapse of Wirecard, Germany's biggest corporate fraud scandal, through reported storytelling.
shows.notableSeasons: Full arc covering Wirecard's rise and collapse
shows.whyNotable: One of the most successful German true-crime/business podcasts ever, widely credited with popularizing narrative podcasting in Germany. Feed note: base feed is free, ad-free/early access via Wondery+.

shows.title: El Extraordinario
shows.description: Spanish-language narrative anthology podcast telling bizarre-but-true stories and extraordinary real-life events through cinematic scripted narration.
shows.whyNotable: Known for high-production narrative storytelling in the Spanish-language podcast scene.

shows.title: Caso 63 (Spanish/Latin American adaptation)
shows.description: Scripted narrative audio-fiction series presented in documentary/found-audio style about a scientist and a psychiatric patient exchanging warnings across time.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 original Chilean/Latin American run
shows.whyNotable: A breakout Latin American scripted-audio hit later adapted into multiple languages worldwide, credited with putting Spanish-language fiction podcasting on the map.

shows.title: Bienvenidos a Racoon City / Poblёt (regional narrative true crime, Spain)
shows.description: Narrative reconstruction in Spanish of a real regional crime case in Spain, told across a serialized season with dramatized reenactment and interviews.
shows.whyNotable: Representative of Podium Podcast's strong slate of Spanish narrative true-crime originals.

shows.title: Reveal en español / Distintas Latitudes narrative specials
shows.network: The Center for Investigative Reporting / Distintas Latitudes
shows.description: Spanish-language narrative investigative journalism specials covering corruption, migration, and human rights across Latin America.
shows.whyNotable: Extends the Peabody-winning Reveal investigative model into Spanish-language narrative reporting.

shows.title: El Gran Apagón (fiction-as-journalism)
shows.description: Scripted speculative-fiction podcast in Spanish presented as a series of found recordings and news-style dispatches from a near-future blackout crisis in Spain.
shows.notableSeasons: Single acclaimed season, later adapted for TV (Apagón)
shows.whyNotable: Landmark Spanish scripted-fiction-as-journalism podcast; later became an HBO Max anthology series.

shows.title: Der Mordfall (working title narrative crime docs)
shows.description: German investigative-narrative podcast from Süddeutsche Zeitung reporters reconstructing real, often decades-old crimes through original reporting.
shows.whyNotable: Produced by one of Germany's most respected investigative newsrooms, extending its print journalism into serialized narrative audio.

shows.title: À qui appartient l'histoire / Sous surveillance
shows.description: French-Canadian narrative documentary podcast series investigating true crime and social issues in Quebec through reported storytelling.
shows.whyNotable: A flagship French-language narrative documentary offering from Canada's public broadcaster.

shows.title: El Cuarto Poder (narrative journalism, Mexico)
shows.description: Spanish-language narrative journalism podcast investigating political corruption and abuse-of-power cases in Mexico through serialized reporting.
shows.whyNotable: Representative of Mexico's growing narrative investigative-podcast scene tackling corruption stories.

shows.title: Land of the Giants
shows.description: Seasonal narrative-history docuseries chronicling how individual tech and media giants — Amazon, Apple, Netflix, Google, Disney, Twitter — built and wielded monopoly-scale power.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1: The Rise of Amazon; "The Twitter Takeover" season on Elon Musk's acquisition
shows.whyNotable: Consistently ranked among top business/tech podcasts; praised for deep reporting on Big Tech consolidation.

shows.title: The Journal
shows.network: The Wall Street Journal / Spotify Studios
shows.description: Daily and special-series narrative business journalism from WSJ reporters, frequently running multi-part investigative arcs into corporate fraud, tech scandal, and financial crime.
shows.notableSeasons: Multi-part series on FTX/Sam Bankman-Fried; Theranos coverage tie-ins
shows.whyNotable: Backed by WSJ's investigative reporting bench; regularly breaks and narrativizes major business-fraud stories.

shows.title: Darknet Diaries
shows.description: True-story documentary series about hackers, breaches, and cybercrime, built from first-person interviews with hackers, victims, and investigators woven into narrated reconstructions.
shows.notableSeasons: Episodes on the Mt. Gox collapse, Colonial Pipeline, and the Bangladesh Bank heist
shows.whyNotable: One of the most popular independent tech-narrative podcasts; widely cited in the security industry.

shows.title: Malicious Life
shows.description: Narrated history-of-cybersecurity series reconstructing infamous hacks, malware outbreaks, and digital espionage operations as serialized true stories.
shows.notableSeasons: Stuxnet arc; the Equifax breach
shows.whyNotable: Long-running, well-reviewed entry in the cybersecurity-narrative niche.

shows.title: Exit Scam
shows.description: Narrative documentary about the mysterious death of QuadrigaCX founder Gerald Cotten and the $250 million in customer crypto that vanished with him.
shows.whyNotable: Widely covered breakout crypto-fraud narrative series.

shows.title: Swindled
shows.description: Independently produced true-story series on corporate fraud, environmental crimes, and con artists, narrated anonymously with heavy investigative research.
shows.notableSeasons: Episodes on Theranos, the Flint water crisis, and Purdue Pharma
shows.whyNotable: Cult following for its meticulous, indie investigative approach to corporate wrongdoing.

shows.title: American Innovations
shows.description: Narrated history series on the inventions, entrepreneurs, and companies that built modern industries, blending business history with science storytelling.
shows.notableSeasons: Seasons on the automobile industry and the space race
shows.whyNotable: Companion series to American Scandal; broad audience for accessible tech/business history.

shows.title: Persona: The French Deception
shows.description: Narrative investigation into a woman who posed as a Rothschild banking heiress to swindle wealthy victims across Europe out of millions.
shows.whyNotable: Notable entry in Wondery's con-artist narrative catalog.

shows.title: 30 for 30 Podcasts
shows.description: Anthology spinoff of ESPN's acclaimed 30 for 30 documentary film series, using deep archival tape and reporting to tell single-subject sports sagas.
shows.notableSeasons: Fantastic Lies (Duke lacrosse case), Bikram (yoga guru's rise and fall), Heavy Medals (Béla Károlyi and USA gymnastics), Fastest Man Alive (Tyson Gay doping scandal), Screwball (Alex Rodriguez/Biogenesis)
shows.whyNotable: Extension of the Peabody- and Sports Emmy-winning 30 for 30 film franchise; Bikram and Fantastic Lies drew wide critical acclaim.

shows.title: American Fiasco
shows.description: Narrative account of the 1998 U.S. Men's National Soccer Team's spectacular clubhouse implosion at the World Cup in France, told through players and staff.
shows.whyNotable: Widely praised as one of the strongest entries in the 30 for 30 Podcasts slate for its intimate access and pacing.

shows.title: Rivals
shows.description: Documentary-style anthology narrating the histories of iconic sports rivalries — franchises, athletes, and fanbases — through archival tape and reporting.
shows.notableSeasons: Yankees–Red Sox, Lakers–Celtics, Ali–Frazier
shows.whyNotable: Flagship narrative sports project from Bill Simmons' The Ringer network.

shows.title: Slow Burn: Biggie & Tupac
shows.description: A season of Slate's Slow Burn franchise reconstructing the unsolved murders of Notorious B.I.G. and Tupac Shakur and the East Coast–West Coast rivalry that framed them.
shows.whyNotable: One of the most acclaimed seasons of the Slow Burn franchise; widely reviewed alongside other Biggie/Tupac cold-case coverage. (Ad-free/early access requires Slate Plus.)

shows.title: Cocaine & Rhinestones
shows.description: Deeply researched narrative history of country music, unpacking the industry battles, personal dramas, and myths behind its biggest stars and songs.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (George Jones/Tammy Wynette, Loretta Lynn, Dolly Parton, Merle Haggard episodes)
shows.whyNotable: Critically lauded for exhaustive, footnoted research; frequently cited as a model for narrative music journalism.

shows.title: The Last Days of August
shows.description: Jon Ronson's narrative investigation into the death of adult-film star August Ames after an online pile-on, examining the culture and business behind it.
shows.whyNotable: From acclaimed narrative journalist Jon Ronson (The Butterfly Effect); noted for sensitive handling of a fraught subject.

shows.title: The Big Hit Show
shows.description: Narrative deep-dives into how specific songs, albums, or shows became runaway cultural hits, tracing the decisions and accidents behind them.
shows.notableSeasons: 'Old Town Road,' Daft Punk's Random Access Memories
shows.whyNotable: Spotify Original hosted by Alex Pappademas; praised for granular music-industry reporting. (Spotify-exclusive — no open RSS.)

shows.title: Cover Up
shows.description: Anthology series investigating hidden or lost mysteries in music history, from missing recordings to buried scandals.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1: Bruce Springsteen's missing Electric Nebraska tapes
shows.whyNotable: Notable for original archival digging into music-industry lore rarely covered elsewhere.

shows.title: Hit Parade
shows.description: Narrated history of the pop charts, tracing how eras, genres, and specific number-one hits reshaped the music industry and American culture.
shows.notableSeasons: Multi-part histories of disco, hip-hop's chart takeover, and one-hit wonders
shows.whyNotable: Hosted by chart historian Chris Molanphy; a Slate flagship narrative-history show. (Ad-free/bonus access requires Slate Plus.)

shows.title: Have You Heard George's Podcast
shows.description: Genre-blending narrative series mixing poetry, jazz, and reported storytelling to explore Black British identity, history, and culture through fictionalized-journalistic vignettes.
shows.whyNotable: Winner of a Peabody Award and British Podcast Awards; praised as boundary-pushing audio art.

shows.title: The Dream
shows.description: Investigative narrative series exposing the multi-level marketing and wellness industries, following the people who profit from and are harmed by them.
shows.notableSeasons: Season 1 (MLMs), Season 2 (the wellness industry)
shows.whyNotable: Widely credited with mainstreaming journalistic scrutiny of the MLM industry. (Early/ad-free access via Stitcher Premium.)

shows.title: Bear Grease
shows.description: Long-form narrative history of American outdoor, hunting, and rural culture, tracing folklore and real events that shaped frontier and sporting traditions.
shows.whyNotable: Became one of the top outdoor-culture podcasts, known for immersive long-form narration.

shows.title: Blindspot: Empire City
shows.network: WNYC Studios / THE CITY / History Channel
shows.description: Narrative history of the NYPD's 175-year rise as an institution of power, tracing its roots in slave patrols, corruption, and reform through reported storytelling.
shows.whyNotable: Part of the award-winning Blindspot investigative-history franchise.

shows.title: Theory of Everything
shows.description: Benjamen Walker fuses documentary reporting, personal essay, and outright fiction into one seamless narrative, deliberately blurring which segments are true — a defining experiment in fiction-as-journalism.
shows.notableSeasons: Instaserfs (2015, 3-part gig-economy exposé reported from inside the sharing economy); The Secret Histories of Podcasting; New York After Rent; False Alarm! / The Dislike Club
shows.whyNotable: Radiotopia flagship and a landmark of genre-blurring audio; evolved from WFMU's Too Much Information. Chris flag: Instaserfs and The Secret Histories of Podcasting are canon.
